codeReview.summary: The admin-tramites-redesign feature implements a polished redesign of the admin procedures page with procedure cards, detail panel, and create modal. All expected behaviors are present: cards show name/slug/active badge with proper selected state styling, detail panel displays full configuration with tabs, toggle isActive works, duplicate creates a copy with modified slug, remove requires confirmation, and create modal has complete form with validation. The implementation follows design system guidelines using adminUi tokens and Mantine components consistently.

issues.description: The docCount calculation uses `as unknown[]` type cast: `(procedure.documentSchema?.requirements as unknown[])?.length ?? 0`. While this pattern is consistent across the feature (also used in ProcedureDetailPanel), it could silently fail if the actual documentSchema structure differs from expectations. Consider adding a runtime check or defining a more specific type.

The slug sanitization function doesn't handle accented characters in the input before processing. The generateSlugFromName function (lines 62-77) does handle accented characters, but sanitizeSlug (lines 47-55) does not. If sanitizeSlug is called directly with accented characters, it may produce unexpected slugs. However, in the current usage flow, this is mitigated because auto-generation uses generateSlugFromName.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed existing patterns correctly. However, AGENTS.md mentions the project uses Mantine + inline styles + src/styles.css for UI, but the new code uses Tailwind CSS classes extensively (e.g., 'gap-3', 'rounded-lg', 'text-zinc-500'). This is not documented as an accepted pattern in AGENTS.md. The architecture.md does mention 'Mantine 9 for UI components' but doesn't specify CSS approach. The code shows Tailwind is being used alongside Mantine, which appears to be an established (if undocumented) pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 4 modified files use Tailwind utility classes extensively alongside Mantine components

sharedStateObservations.observation: The impeccable-admin-worker skill was used. The worker followed the procedure correctly as indicated by skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true. The transcript shows the worker read mission.md, AGENTS.md, validation-contract.md, features.json and then proceeded to implement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback shows followedProcedure: true with no deviations

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services were started that should be documented. The worker only ran existing commands (bun run test, bun run lint) which are already in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands run were: 'bun run test', 'bun run lint' - all in services.yaml

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new knowledge gaps discovered. The worker understood the existing codebase patterns and used existing components (adminUi, FormSection, PremiumModal, FormActionButton, etc.) correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Code shows proper usage of shared admin components and design tokens

summary: Feature admin-tramites-redesign passes review. The redesign implements all required behaviors: procedure cards with name/slug/active badge, distinct selected state styling, full detail panel with tabs for configuration, isActive toggle, duplicate with modified slug, remove with confirmation, and create modal with complete validated form. The implementation is consistent with existing design patterns using adminUi tokens and Mantine components. Two minor non-blocking observations: type cast pattern for docCount and slug sanitization edge case, neither of which blocks functionality.
